## UNIT: Plan Slice S05 ("Mobile Polish + Design System Pass") — Milestone M001

All relevant context has been preloaded below — start working immediately without re-reading these files.

## Inlined Context (preloaded — do not re-read these files)

### Milestone Roadmap
Source: `.gsd/milestones/M001/M001-ROADMAP.md`

# M001: Launch-Ready Oriflect Website

**Vision:** Convert completed HTML mockups into a navigable, mobile-responsive React + Vite site deployable to Vercel and Lovable, with ROI calculator and client portal concept added.

## Success Criteria

- All 7 pages render with correct Axiom Navy design system
- React Router navigation works with no full-page reloads
- Active nav state highlights current page
- Mobile hamburger menu opens/closes correctly
- Contact form shows success state on submit
- ROI calculator computes and displays results
- `npm run build` produces clean dist/ with no errors

## Key Risks / Unknowns

- Tailwind token extraction from CDN config — hyphens in color names must survive
- lh3.googleusercontent.com image URLs may 403 in some environments

## Proof Strategy

- Token extraction risk → retire in S01 by running `npm run dev` and visually confirming colors render
- Image URLs → use as-is; note in UAT for user to replace with real assets

## Verification Classes

- Contract verification: `npm run build` passes, all routes render without 404
- Integration verification: React Router `<Link>` navigation tested across all pages
- UAT / human verification: visual design check against mockup screenshots

## Milestone Definition of Done

- All 7 pages navigable via React Router
- Shared Nav + Footer on every page
- Active nav state correct per page
- Mobile responsive hamburger works
- Contact form and newsletter show feedback states
- ROI calculator functional
- `npm run build` clean
- vercel.json present for SPA routing

## Requirement Coverage

- Covers: R001, R002, R003, R004, R005, R006, R007, R008, R009, R010, R011
- Out of scope: R012, R013, R014

## Slices

- [x] **S01: Vite + React + Tailwind Scaffold** `risk:high` `depends:[]`
  > After this: `npm run dev` serves a blank Oriflect shell at localhost:5173 with all design tokens loaded and fonts rendering

- [x] **S02: Shared Nav + Footer Components** `risk:medium` `depends:[S01]`
  > After this: Nav and Footer render on a test page; nav links highlight active state; mobile hamburger opens/closes

- [x] **S03: Home + Services Pages** `risk:medium` `depends:[S02]`
  > After this: Home and Services pages fully rendered from mockup content with working nav

- [x] **S04: About + Blog + Contact Pages** `risk:medium` `depends:[S02]`
  > After this: All 5 core pages navigable; contact form and newsletter show success feedback

- [ ] **S05: Mobile Polish + Design System Pass** `risk:low` `depends:[S03,S04]`
  > After this: Site is fully responsive at all breakpoints; design system consistent with DESIGN.md spec

- [x] **S06: ROI Calculator + Client Portal Page** `risk:medium` `depends:[S04]`
  > After this: /roi page has working calculator with real computation; /portal renders concept dashboard
  > Note: Built pre-emptively before S04 completed. ROICalculator.jsx (298 lines) and ClientPortal.jsx (276 lines) exist and are routed. Verified via `npm run build` clean pass.

## Boundary Map

### S01 → S02
Produces:
- Vite + React project at project root
- `tailwind.config.js` with full Axiom Navy token set
- `src/main.jsx` entry point
- `src/App.jsx` with React Router setup (placeholder routes)
- `src/index.css` with base styles + font imports

Consumes:
- nothing (first slice)

### S01 → S03, S04, S05, S06
Produces:
- All of the above

### S02 → S03, S04, S05, S06
Produces:
- `src/components/Nav.jsx` — sticky nav with active state via useLocation
- `src/components/Footer.jsx` — shared footer
- `src/components/Layout.jsx` — wraps Nav + children + Footer

Consumes from S01:
- Tailwind config tokens
- React Router context

### S03 → S05
Produces:
- `src/pages/Home.jsx`
- `src/pages/Services.jsx`

### S04 → S05, S06
Produces:
- `src/pages/About.jsx`
- `src/pages/Blog.jsx`
- `src/pages/Contact.jsx` with form state
- Newsletter subscribe state in Blog.jsx

### S05 → S06
Produces:
- Responsive polish applied to all pages
- No new interfaces, just quality

### S06 (terminal)
Produces:
- `src/pages/ROICalculator.jsx` — stateful calculator
- `src/pages/ClientPortal.jsx` — concept dashboard
- Routes /roi and /portal registered in App.jsx

---

### Slice Research
Source: `.gsd/milestones/M001/slices/S05/S05-RESEARCH.md`

# S05: Mobile Polish + Design System Pass — Research

**Date:** 2026-03-25

## Summary

S05 is a polish-only slice — no new pages, no new routes. All 7 pages exist and build cleanly. The design system tokens are well-established in `tailwind.config.js` and `index.css`. The Nav hamburger is already implemented and functional. The main work is auditing all pages at mobile breakpoints for overflow/clipping issues, fixing oversized type, and ensuring design system consistency (0px border-radius throughout, ghost borders, correct surface tokens).

The biggest concrete risk is large display type rendering at mobile sizes. Several pages use `text-6xl` or `text-7xl` as the *mobile-first* (base) class before a `md:` responsive variant — these will overflow or force horizontal scroll on 375px screens. Contact uses `text-7xl` with no smaller mobile equivalent; Services header uses `text-6xl` as base. These need stepped-down type scales at `sm:` or as base classes.

The design system is internally consistent — Tailwind tokens, utility classes (`glass-header`, `grid-bg`, `ghost-border`), and font families are all applied correctly across pages. No token drift detected. The main design-system pass is verification, not repair.

## Recommendation

Work page-by-page: spin up `npm run dev`, resize to 375px in browser devtools, identify overflow/clipping, fix class by class. Don't restructure layouts — just add responsive prefixes where base sizes are too large. After mobile fixes, do a single pass for design consistency (border-radius, token usage, spacing rhythm).

## Existing Code and Patterns

- `oriflect/src/components/Nav.jsx` — hamburger already works; `max-h-screen` transition on mobile menu is functional; close-on-route-change via `useEffect` is in place. No changes needed unless visual QA reveals issues.
- `oriflect/src/index.css` — `glass-header`, `grid-bg`, `ghost-border`, `ghost-grid`, `hero-gradient` utilities all defined. These are the correct patterns to use in pages; don't inline duplicate styles.
- `oriflect/tailwind.config.js` — all tokens present; border-radius overrides zero out all rounding except `full`. Safe assumption: any `rounded-*` class in page code (except `rounded-full`) is a bug — grep for these.
- `oriflect/src/pages/Home.jsx` — good responsive pattern: `text-5xl md:text-7xl lg:text-8xl`. Use this as the reference pattern for other pages.

## Constraints

- No new pages, routes, or components — S05 boundary is quality only
- Must not break the `npm run build` (currently passing per S06 note in roadmap)
- Keep plain JS / JSX — no TypeScript
- Tailwind v3 — no v4 syntax (no `@theme`, no bare CSS variable tokens)

## Common Pitfalls

- **Bare `text-6xl`/`text-7xl`/`text-9xl` as base class** — these render ~60–72px on mobile and cause horizontal scroll. Fix: add `text-4xl sm:text-6xl md:text-9xl` stepped scale.
- **`max-w-md` inside flex on mobile** — `max-w-md` (448px) exceeds 375px viewport; works fine on desktop but clips on mobile. Fix: add `w-full` alongside or drop max-w at mobile.
- **`px-24` without `md:` prefix** — if any section uses `px-24` without `md:` it will eat 96px of gutter on mobile. Check all pages; `px-8 md:px-24` is the correct pattern already used on Home.
- **`min-h-[90vh]` hero on mobile** — fine, but ensure content doesn't overflow on small screens without scrolling; test with real content.
- **Material Symbols `text-9xl`** — `Services.jsx:178` uses `text-9xl` on an icon inside a panel. At mobile this is 128px tall and may force vertical reflow. May need `md:text-9xl text-6xl`.

## Open Risks

- `lh3.googleusercontent.com` images: known risk from M001 context. Not in S05 scope to fix, but worth noting for UAT — if images 403 during visual QA, layout should still hold (test with images blocked).
- Services page layout uses `lg:grid-cols-12` not `md:` — at tablet (768–1023px) the page will render single-column. This may be intentional or may feel sparse. Worth a visual check at `md` breakpoint.

## Skills Discovered

| Technology | Skill | Status |
|------------|-------|--------|
| React / Tailwind responsive | (none needed — standard knowledge) | n/a |

## Specific Issues to Fix (pre-diagnosed)

| File | Line(s) | Issue | Fix |
|------|---------|-------|-----|
| `Contact.jsx` | ~73 | `text-7xl md:text-9xl` — base 7xl is still huge on mobile | Change to `text-4xl sm:text-6xl md:text-9xl` |
| `Services.jsx` | ~251 | `text-6xl md:text-9xl` header — same | `text-4xl sm:text-6xl md:text-9xl` |
| `About.jsx` | ~50 | `text-6xl md:text-8xl` | `text-4xl sm:text-6xl md:text-8xl` |
| `Blog.jsx` | ~87 | `text-6xl md:text-8xl` | `text-4xl sm:text-6xl md:text-8xl` |
| `Services.jsx` | ~178 | `material-symbols-outlined text-9xl` (icon) | `text-5xl md:text-9xl` |
| All pages | — | Audit for `rounded-*` (non-full) classes — 0px radius is a design system rule | `rg 'rounded-[^f]' src/pages` |
| All pages | — | Audit for `px-24` without `md:` prefix | `rg 'px-24[^/]' src/pages` |

## Sources

- Codebase audit: `oriflect/src/pages/*.jsx`, `oriflect/src/components/Nav.jsx`, `oriflect/tailwind.config.js`, `oriflect/src/index.css`
- Design system: `stitch_oriflect_landing_page/axiom_navy/DESIGN.md` (preloaded context)
- Requirements: R004 (design system), R005 (mobile hamburger)

# Decisions Register

<!-- Append-only. Never edit or remove existing rows. -->

| # | When | Scope | Decision | Choice | Rationale | Revisable? |
|---|------|-------|----------|--------|-----------|------------|
| D001 | M001 | arch | Frontend framework | React + Vite | Lovable runs React natively; Vercel deploys Vite out of the box; no build complexity | No |
| D002 | M001 | library | CSS framework | Tailwind CSS via npm | Extracted from CDN config in mockups; needed for tree-shaking in production build | No |
| D003 | M001 | library | Routing | React Router v6 | Standard for React SPAs; Vercel SPA routing via vercel.json | No |
| D004 | M001 | convention | No TypeScript | Plain JS (.jsx) | Simpler for Lovable import and visual editing; user did not request TS | Yes — if Lovable supports TS |
| D005 | M001 | arch | Form handling | Client-side only with simulated success state | No backend in scope; forms show feedback without submission | Yes — when backend added |
| D006 | M001 | convention | Images | Keep lh3.googleusercontent.com URLs from mockups | Fastest path to launch; user can replace with real assets later | Yes — production asset replacement |
| D007 | S01 | verification | S01 verification strategy | Build + grep assertions (no test framework) | S01 is a scaffold slice — contract proof via `npm run build` + token grep is sufficient; no UI logic to unit test | Yes — introduce Playwright in S02 or S05 if visual regression needed |
| D008 | S01 | convention | Project subdirectory | Vite project at `oriflect/` not workspace root | Scaffold was created pre-GSD; deviation is acceptable; all S02+ work targets `oriflect/src/` | No |

Narrate your decomposition reasoning — why you're grouping work this way, what risks are driving the order, what verification strategy you're choosing and why.

Then:
0. If `REQUIREMENTS.md` was preloaded above, identify which Active requirements the roadmap says this slice owns or supports. These are the requirements this plan must deliver — every owned requirement needs at least one task that directly advances it, and verification must prove the requirement is met.
1. Read the templates:
   - `~/.gsd/agent/extensions/gsd/templates/plan.md`
   - `~/.gsd/agent/extensions/gsd/templates/task-plan.md`
2. If a `GSD Skill Preferences` block is present in system context, use it to decide which skills to load and follow during planning, without overriding required plan formatting
3. Define slice-level verification first — the objective stopping condition for this slice:
   - For non-trivial slices: plan actual test files with real assertions. Name the files. The first task creates them (initially failing). Remaining tasks make them pass.
   - For simple slices: executable commands or script assertions are fine.
   - If the project is non-trivial and has no test framework, the first task should set one up.
   - If this slice establishes a boundary contract, verification must exercise that contract.
4. Plan observability and diagnostics explicitly:
   - For non-trivial backend, integration, async, stateful, or UI slices, include an `Observability / Diagnostics` section in the slice plan.
   - Define how a future agent will inspect state, detect failure, and localize the problem.
   - Prefer structured logs/events, stable error codes/types, status surfaces, and persisted failure state over ad hoc debug text.
   - Include at least one verification check for a diagnostic or failure-path signal when relevant.
5. Fill the `Proof Level` and `Integration Closure` sections truthfully:
   - State whether the slice proves contract, integration, operational, or final-assembly behavior.
   - Say whether real runtime or human/UAT is required.
   - Name the wiring introduced in this slice and what still remains before the milestone is truly usable end-to-end.
6. Decompose the slice into tasks, each fitting one context window
7. Every task in the slice plan should be written as an executable increment with:
   - a concrete, action-oriented title
   - the inline task entry fields defined in the plan.md template (Why / Files / Do / Verify / Done when)
   - a matching task plan containing description, steps, must-haves, verification, observability impact, inputs, and expected output
8. Each task needs: title, description, steps, must-haves, verification, observability impact, inputs, and expected output
9. If verification includes test files, ensure the first task includes creating them with expected assertions (they should fail initially — that's correct)
10. Write `.gsd/milestones/M001/slices/S05/S05-PLAN.md`
11. Write individual task plans in `C:\Users\edwar\Desktop\Oriflect Site\.gsd\milestones\M001\slices\S05/tasks/`: `T01-PLAN.md`, `T02-PLAN.md`, etc.
12. **Self-audit the plan before continuing.** Walk through each check — if any fail, fix the plan files before moving on:
    - **Completion semantics:** If every task were completed exactly as written, the slice goal/demo should actually be true at the claimed proof level. Do not allow a task plan that only scaffolds toward a future working state.
    - **Requirement coverage:** Every must-have in the slice maps to at least one task. No must-have is orphaned.
    - **Task completeness:** Every task has steps, must-haves, verification, observability impact, inputs, and expected output — none are blank or vague.
    - **Dependency correctness:** Task ordering is consistent. No task references work from a later task.
    - **Key links planned:** For every pair of artifacts that must connect (component → API, API → database, form → handler), there is an explicit step that wires them — not just "create X" and "create Y" in separate tasks with no connection step.
    - **Scope sanity:** Target 2–5 steps and 3–8 files per task. 6–8 steps or 8–10 files is a warning — consider splitting. 10+ steps or 12+ files — must split. Each task must be completable in a single fresh context window.
    - **Context compliance:** If context/research artifacts or `.gsd/DECISIONS.md` exist, the plan honors locked decisions and doesn't include deferred or out-of-scope items.
    - **Requirement coverage:** If `REQUIREMENTS.md` exists, every Active requirement this slice owns (per the roadmap) maps to at least one task with verification that proves the requirement is met. No owned requirement is left without a task. No task claims to satisfy a requirement that is Deferred or Out of Scope.
    - **Proof honesty:** The `Proof Level` and `Integration Closure` sections match what this slice will actually prove, and they do not imply live end-to-end completion if only fixture or contract proof is planned.
    - **Feature completeness:** Every task produces real, user-facing progress — not just internal scaffolding. If the slice has a UI surface, at least one task builds the real UI (not a placeholder). If the slice has an API, at least one task connects it to a real data source (not hardcoded returns). If every task were completed and you showed the result to a non-technical stakeholder, they should see real product progress, not developer artifacts.
13. If planning produced structural decisions (e.g. verification strategy, observability strategy, technology choices, patterns to follow), append them to `.gsd/DECISIONS.md`
14. Commit: `docs(S05): add slice plan`
15. Update `.gsd/STATE.md`

The slice directory and tasks/ subdirectory already exist. Do NOT mkdir. You are on the slice branch; all work stays here.

**You MUST write the file `C:\Users\edwar\Desktop\Oriflect Site\.gsd\milestones\M001\slices\S05\S05-PLAN.md` before finishing.**

When done, say: "Slice S05 planned."

     - Verify not just that the feature works, but that a future agent can inspect its state when it fails -->

- {{testFileOrCommand — e.g. `npm test -- --grep "auth flow"` or `bash scripts/verify-s01.sh`}}
- {{testFileOrCommand}}

## Observability / Diagnostics

<!-- Required for non-trivial backend, integration, async, stateful, or UI slices.
     Describe how a future agent will inspect current state, detect failure,
     and localize the problem with minimal ambiguity.

     Prefer:
     - structured logs/events over ad hoc console strings
     - stable error codes/types over vague failures
     - health/readiness/status surfaces over hidden internal state
     - persisted failure state when it materially improves retries or recovery

     Keep this section concise and high-signal. Do not log secrets or sensitive raw payloads. -->

## Integration Closure

- New wiring introduced in this slice: {{entrypoint / composition / runtime hookup, or none}}
- What remains before the milestone is truly usable end-to-end: {{list or "nothing"}}

## Tasks

  If every task below is completed exactly as written, the Goal and Demo above
  should be true at the stated proof level. Tasks should close the loop on the
  slice, not merely prepare for later work unless the Demo truthfully says the
  slice only proves fixture/contract-level behavior.

  Write each task as an executable increment, not a vague intention.

  Prefer action-oriented titles:
  - "Wire real auth middleware into dashboard routes"
  - "Persist job status and expose failure diagnostics"
  - "Add browser test covering empty-state recovery"

  Avoid vague titles:

  - "Handle errors"

  Each task should usually include:

  - Files: the main files likely touched

  - Verify: the command, test, or runtime check that proves it worked
  - Done when: a measurable acceptance condition

  Keep the checkbox line format exactly:
  - [ ] **T01: Title** `est:30m`
-->

content.thinking:
Now I have the templates. Let me think through the decomposition for S05.

**Requirements owned by S05:**
- R004 (Axiom Navy design system) — supporting slice
- R005 (Mobile-responsive hamburger nav) — **primary owning slice**

**What S05 does:**
- Fix oversized mobile type across Contact, Services, About, Blog pages
- Audit and remove any `rounded-*` (non-full) classes
- Audit `px-24` without `md:` prefix
- Fix Services icon `text-9xl` → `text-5xl md:text-9xl`


- Verify hamburger nav works at mobile and run visual QA across all breakpoints

The core work is fixing typography scaling and design system compliance, then validating with a build check and manual browser testing at mobile widths since responsive rendering can't be caught by automated tests.

I'm settling on two tasks: one to handle all the code fixes—typography, icon scaling, and design system audit—and another to verify the build passes and do a visual check at 375px.

content.text:
Decomposition reasoning: S05 is purely a polish slice with pre-diagnosed issues. No new components, no new routes. The work splits cleanly into two tasks: (1) apply all known code fixes — typography scaling, icon sizing, design system audit; (2) verify the build, run grep assertions to confirm no banned patterns remain, and do a visual browser check at 375px. This ordering ensures fixes come first, then a single verification pass rather than piecemeal checking.

Verification strategy: no test framework makes sense here — the assertions are grep (banned patterns → 0 hits) plus `npm run build` (regression) plus browser screenshot at 375px (proof of no horizontal overflow). Simple commands are the right tool.

Requirements coverage: R005 (mobile hamburger) is the primary owned requirement; R004 (design system) is supported. Both are addressable within these two tasks.

arguments.content:
# S05: Mobile Polish + Design System Pass

**Goal:** All 7 pages are fully responsive at 375px–1440px with no horizontal overflow; design system is consistent (0px border-radius, correct token usage, stepped type scale); `npm run build` passes clean.
**Demo:** Resize devtools to 375px — every page scrolls vertically with no horizontal overflow, hero text is readable (not overflowing), hamburger menu opens and closes correctly, no rounded corners appear on surface cards.

## Must-Haves

- Fix oversized base mobile type on Contact, Services, About, Blog pages (pre-diagnosed)
- Fix `text-9xl` icon on Services page (pre-diagnosed)
- `npm run build` passes with no errors
- No `rounded-*` (except `rounded-full`) in any `src/pages/*.jsx` file
- No `px-24` without `md:` prefix in any page file
- Hamburger nav opens and closes at 375px
- No horizontal scroll on any page at 375px viewport

## Proof Level

- This slice proves: operational
- Real runtime required: yes (browser visual check)
- Human/UAT required: yes (visual sign-off on all pages)

## Verification

- `cd oriflect && npm run build` — exits 0 with no errors
- `rg 'rounded-(?!full)' oriflect/src/pages/` — 0 results
- `rg '\bpx-24\b(?!.*md:)' oriflect/src/pages/` — 0 results (or all matches have responsive prefix)
- Browser at 375px: no horizontal scrollbar on any page; hero text readable; hamburger works

## Observability / Diagnostics

- Runtime signals: build output — any JSX syntax error will surface here before browser check
- Inspection surfaces: `npm run build` stdout; browser devtools device emulation at 375px
- Failure visibility: if a page overflows, Chrome devtools scroll detection shows red border on overflowing element; `npm run build` will catch syntax errors before visual check
- Redaction constraints: none

## Integration Closure

- Upstream surfaces consumed: `oriflect/src/pages/*.jsx`, `oriflect/src/components/Nav.jsx`, `oriflect/tailwind.config.js`, `oriflect/src/index.css`
- New wiring introduced in this slice: none — responsive class changes only
- What remains before the milestone is truly usable end-to-end: nothing — this is the final quality pass before milestone close

## Tasks

- [ ] **T01: Fix mobile typography, icon sizing, and design system compliance across all pages** `est:45m`
  - Why: Pre-diagnosed oversized type on Contact/Services/About/Blog will overflow at 375px; Services icon at text-9xl forces vertical reflow; any rounded-* or bare px-24 violates the 0px border-radius design system rule
  - Files: `oriflect/src/pages/Contact.jsx`, `oriflect/src/pages/Services.jsx`, `oriflect/src/pages/About.jsx`, `oriflect/src/pages/Blog.jsx`
  - Do:
    1. In `Contact.jsx` ~line 73: change `text-7xl md:text-9xl` → `text-4xl sm:text-6xl md:text-9xl`
    2. In `Services.jsx` ~line 251: change `text-6xl md:text-9xl` → `text-4xl sm:text-6xl md:text-9xl`
    3. In `Services.jsx` ~line 178: change `material-symbols-outlined text-9xl` → `material-symbols-outlined text-5xl md:text-9xl`
    4. In `About.jsx` ~line 50: change `text-6xl md:text-8xl` → `text-4xl sm:text-6xl md:text-8xl`
    5. In `Blog.jsx` ~line 87: change `text-6xl md:text-8xl` → `text-4xl sm:text-6xl md:text-8xl`
    6. Run `rg 'rounded-(?!full)' oriflect/src/pages/` — remove or replace any hits (do not add rounded classes; delete them or replace with `rounded-none` if explicit reset is needed)
    7. Run `rg '\bpx-24\b' oriflect/src/pages/` — for any bare `px-24` (not prefixed with `md:`), change to `px-8 md:px-24`
    8. Also audit ROICalculator.jsx and ClientPortal.jsx for the same patterns
  - Verify: `rg 'rounded-(?!full)' oriflect/src/pages/` returns 0 results; `rg 'text-7xl|text-6xl|text-9xl' oriflect/src/pages/` shows only results that have a smaller sm:/base class preceding them
  - Done when: All pre-diagnosed type fixes applied, grep audits pass (0 rounded-* hits, 0 bare px-24 hits)

- [ ] **T02: Build verification and mobile visual QA** `est:30m`
  - Why: Confirms no regressions from T01 edits and proves R005 (mobile hamburger) and R004 (design system) at operational level
  - Files: none modified — verification only
  - Do:
    1. Run `cd oriflect && npm run build` — must exit 0
    2. Start dev server: `cd oriflect && npm run dev`
    3. Open browser at http://localhost:5173, set devtools viewport to 375px
    4. Visit all 7 routes: `/`, `/services`, `/about`, `/blog`, `/contact`, `/roi`, `/portal`
    5. On each page: confirm no horizontal scrollbar, hero text is readable (not clipped), content stacks vertically
    6. On any page: open hamburger menu — confirm it opens; click a link — confirm it closes; resize back to 1280px — confirm desktop nav returns
    7. Screenshot at least Home and Contact at 375px for UAT record
  - Verify: `npm run build` exits 0; browser shows no horizontal overflow on any of the 7 pages; hamburger opens/closes
  - Done when: Build clean, visual check passes all 7 pages at 375px, hamburger confirmed functional

## Files Likely Touched

- `oriflect/src/pages/Contact.jsx`
- `oriflect/src/pages/Services.jsx`
- `oriflect/src/pages/About.jsx`
- `oriflect/src/pages/Blog.jsx`
- `oriflect/src/pages/ROICalculator.jsx` (audit only — fix if issues found)
- `oriflect/src/pages/ClientPortal.jsx` (audit only — fix if issues found)

---
estimated_steps: 8
estimated_files: 6
---

# T01: Fix mobile typography, icon sizing, and design system compliance across all pages

**Slice:** S05 — Mobile Polish + Design System Pass
**Milestone:** M001

## Description

Apply pre-diagnosed responsive class fixes to Contact, Services, About, and Blog pages. Step down oversized base mobile type sizes that cause horizontal scroll at 375px. Shrink the Services icon from text-9xl to a mobile-safe size. Audit all page files for banned `rounded-*` (non-full) classes and bare `px-24` without responsive prefix, and remove/fix any hits. Also audit ROICalculator and ClientPortal.

## Steps

1. Open `oriflect/src/pages/Contact.jsx` — find `text-7xl md:text-9xl` and change to `text-4xl sm:text-6xl md:text-9xl`
2. Open `oriflect/src/pages/Services.jsx` — find `text-6xl md:text-9xl` header (~line 251) and change to `text-4xl sm:text-6xl md:text-9xl`; find `material-symbols-outlined text-9xl` icon (~line 178) and change to `material-symbols-outlined text-5xl md:text-9xl`
3. Open `oriflect/src/pages/About.jsx` — find `text-6xl md:text-8xl` and change to `text-4xl sm:text-6xl md:text-8xl`
4. Open `oriflect/src/pages/Blog.jsx` — find `text-6xl md:text-8xl` and change to `text-4xl sm:text-6xl md:text-8xl`
5. Run `rg 'rounded-(?!full)' oriflect/src/pages/` — for each hit, remove the rounded class or replace with `rounded-none` if an explicit override is semantically needed
6. Run `rg '\bpx-24\b' oriflect/src/pages/` — for any bare `px-24` not paired with a `md:` prefix on the same element, change to `px-8 md:px-24`
7. Apply same rounded/px-24 audit to `oriflect/src/pages/ROICalculator.jsx` and `oriflect/src/pages/ClientPortal.jsx`
8. Run `rg 'rounded-(?!full)' oriflect/src/pages/` again — confirm 0 results

## Must-Haves

- [ ] Contact hero type changed from `text-7xl` base to `text-4xl` base
- [ ] Services header type changed from `text-6xl` base to `text-4xl` base
- [ ] Services material icon changed from `text-9xl` to `text-5xl md:text-9xl`
- [ ] About hero type changed from `text-6xl` base to `text-4xl` base
- [ ] Blog hero type changed from `text-6xl` base to `text-4xl` base
- [ ] 0 `rounded-*` (non-full) classes in any `src/pages/*.jsx` file
- [ ] 0 bare `px-24` (without `md:` prefix) in any page file

## Verification

- `rg 'rounded-(?!full)' oriflect/src/pages/` → 0 results
- `rg 'class="[^"]*\bpx-24\b(?![^"]*md:)' oriflect/src/pages/` → 0 results (or inspect manually)
- Visually scan the 5 changed files to confirm the stepped scale pattern matches the Home.jsx reference: `text-5xl md:text-7xl lg:text-8xl`

## Observability Impact

- Signals added/changed: None — these are static class changes with no runtime state
- How a future agent inspects this: grep the files for the banned patterns; diff against this plan's before/after
- Failure state exposed: If build fails after edits, syntax error will be visible in `npm run build` stderr

## Inputs

- `oriflect/src/pages/Contact.jsx` — pre-diagnosed `text-7xl md:text-9xl` at ~line 73
- `oriflect/src/pages/Services.jsx` — pre-diagnosed `text-6xl md:text-9xl` at ~line 251 and `text-9xl` icon at ~line 178
- `oriflect/src/pages/About.jsx` — pre-diagnosed `text-6xl md:text-8xl` at ~line 50
- `oriflect/src/pages/Blog.jsx` — pre-diagnosed `text-6xl md:text-8xl` at ~line 87
- S05-RESEARCH.md issue table — authoritative list of pre-diagnosed problems

## Expected Output

- `oriflect/src/pages/Contact.jsx` — updated with stepped mobile type scale
- `oriflect/src/pages/Services.jsx` — updated with stepped mobile type scale and smaller mobile icon
- `oriflect/src/pages/About.jsx` — updated with stepped mobile type scale
- `oriflect/src/pages/Blog.jsx` — updated with stepped mobile type scale
- Clean grep results: 0 banned rounded/px-24 patterns across all page files

---
estimated_steps: 7
estimated_files: 0
---

# T02: Build verification and mobile visual QA

**Slice:** S05 — Mobile Polish + Design System Pass
**Milestone:** M001

## Description

Prove that all T01 edits are regression-free (clean build) and that the site is genuinely usable at 375px mobile width. This is the operational proof for R005 (hamburger nav) and R004 (design system) — no code changes, verification only.

## Steps

1. Run `cd oriflect && npm run build` — confirm exits 0, no errors in output
2. Start dev server: `cd oriflect && npm run dev` (or confirm it's already running)
3. Open browser at http://localhost:5173 with devtools viewport set to 375px
4. Visit all 7 routes in order: `/`, `/services`, `/about`, `/blog`, `/contact`, `/roi`, `/portal` — on each, confirm: (a) no horizontal scrollbar, (b) hero/section text is readable within viewport, (c) content stacks vertically without clipping
5. On any page, click the hamburger icon — confirm menu opens and shows nav links; click a link — confirm menu closes and page navigates
6. Resize to 1280px — confirm desktop nav (horizontal link bar) returns and hamburger icon is hidden
7. Take a screenshot of Home at 375px and Contact at 375px — attach to S05 UAT record

## Must-Haves

- [ ] `npm run build` exits 0 with no errors
- [ ] No horizontal scroll visible on any of the 7 pages at 375px
- [ ] Hero text on Home, Services, About, Blog, Contact is readable (not overflowing viewport) at 375px
- [ ] Hamburger menu opens on click at 375px; closes on nav link click
- [ ] Desktop nav visible at 1280px; hamburger hidden at 1280px

## Verification

- `npm run build` stdout shows "built in Xs" with exit code 0
- Browser screenshot at 375px shows no horizontal scrollbar for Home and Contact (minimum)
- Hamburger menu interaction confirmed via browser tool

## Observability Impact

- Signals added/changed: None — read-only verification pass
- How a future agent inspects this: re-run `npm run build`; re-open browser at 375px
- Failure state exposed: build failure will show exact JSX line; overflow will be visible as horizontal scrollbar in browser

## Inputs

- `oriflect/src/pages/*.jsx` — all fixed in T01
- `oriflect/src/components/Nav.jsx` — hamburger implementation (no changes expected)
- Running dev server at http://localhost:5173

## Expected Output

- Passing build (no dist/ artifacts need to be committed — just verified)
- Browser visual confirmation that 375px is clean across all 7 routes
- Hamburger nav confirmed functional at mobile viewport
- S05 slice marked complete

content.text:
Self-audit:
- **Completion semantics**: T01 applies all pre-diagnosed fixes; T02 proves them at operational level. If both complete as written, the demo is true. ✓
- **Requirement coverage**: R005 (hamburger) → T02 verifies it; R004 (design system) → T01 fixes rounded/token issues, T02 verifies. ✓
- **Task completeness**: Both tasks have steps, must-haves, verification, observability, inputs, expected output. ✓
- **Dependency correctness**: T01 before T02. ✓
- **Scope sanity**: T01=8 steps/6 files, T02=7 steps/0 files — within limits. ✓
- **Context compliance**: No TypeScript, no new routes, no test framework (simple grep/build assertions are correct for a polish slice). ✓
- **Proof honesty**: Operational proof, real runtime + human/UAT required — honest. ✓
- **Feature completeness**: Both tasks produce real user-facing progress (readable mobile pages). ✓
